top_signals.title: Defense stocks plummet on report Germany is scrapping warships; Rheinmetall stock down 17%
top_signals.summary: Berlin is planning to scrap a multi-billion-euro project to build the F126 frigates, according to reports.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Stock Futures Churn in Run-Up to Micron Earnings: Markets Wrap
top_signals.summary: US stocks ticked higher as investors looked ahead to Micron Technology Inc.’s results for the next read on whether artificial intelligence spending is living up to expectations.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Stock Rout Pauses, Europe Heat Wave Intensifies | The Pulse 6/24/2026
top_signals.summary:
"The Pulse With Francine Lacqua" is all about conversations with high profile guests in the beating heart of global business, economics, finance and politics. Based in London, we go wherever the story is, bringing you exclusive interviews and market-moving scoops.

Today's guests: Joyce Chang, JPMorgan Global Research Chair; Clemens Fuest, Ifo Institute President; Miljan Gutovic, Holcim CEO; Benoit Bazin, Saint-Gobain CEO.    (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Deutsche Bank cuts Q3 gold price target by 22%
top_signals.summary: The German investment bank said it now sees gold averaging $4,300/oz. in the third quarter of this year.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: France suffers major power outage as Europe sizzles in record-breaking heat
top_signals.summary: A heat-related power outage left around 68,000 homes without electricity in western France as record-breaking heat sweeps Europe.

top_signals.title: England draw with Ghana worth £20m extra to British pubs
top_signals.summary: England’s lacklustre 0-0 draw against Ghana may have lacked fizz but hospitality industry experts estimate it was worth an extra £20m in trade for Britain’s pubs. Thomas Tuchel’s men were held to a goalless 90 minutes in Boston on Tuesday but the late kick-off and blistering weather proved a boon to boozers up and down [...]

top_signals.title: Burnham’s new Chief of Staff ran City firm advising Thames Water and rival Heathrow bidder
top_signals.summary: Andy Burnham’s likely chief of staff will move to Downing Street from a lobbying firm that counts Thames Water and the rival bidder for Heathrow’s third runway as clients, just as ministers mull decisions that could decide both organisations’ fate. James Purnell is widely tipped to become Burnham’s most senior right-hand man should the Makerfield [...]

top_signals.title: Aclara Resources gets environmental OK for Chile rare earths project
top_signals.summary: It wraps up a process that has taken longer than four years for the project.
